sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Manchester United have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, securing 3 wins but suffering 2 losses, including a heavy 3-0 defeat to Manchester City. Brighton & Hove Albion, on the other hand, are in fine form with an unbeaten streak of 5 matches, including a dominant 6-0 win over Barnsley and a late 3-1 victory against Chelsea.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Brighton have had the upper hand in recent encounters, winning 4 of the last 5 head-to-head matches against Manchester United, including a 3-1 victory earlier this season at Old Trafford. Their latest meeting on January 19, 2025, saw Brighton dominate with a clinical performance.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Manchester United, positioned 9th with 13 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 12 goals (13th in the league), though they rank well in big chances created (5th). Brighton, sitting 10th with 12 points, have a similar goals scored tally (12) and excel in late-game scoring, with a high percentage of goals in the 76-90 minute range.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: United's strength lies in creating opportunities (high penalty area entries and crosses) but poor conversion rates (11th) might hinder them. Brighton’s ability to score late and their recent form suggest they could exploit United’s defensive frailties, especially with key players like Danny Welbeck in form.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Brighton’s superior head-to-head record, current unbeaten run, and Sofascore’s prediction of a 48% chance for an away win, they appear to have the edge. Manchester United will need to capitalize on home advantage at Old Trafford to turn the tide.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Manchester United lead Brighton & Hove Albion by a comfortable 2-0 scoreline at Old Trafford. The home side has been clinical in front of goal, with Matheus Cunha and Casemiro finding the net in the 24th and 34th minutes respectively. Brighton, despite having more ball possession, have failed to convert their opportunities, leaving them with a mountain to climb in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attacking momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Manchester United started the game with strong attacking intent, dominating the early stages with several promising spells. Brighton responded with a sustained period of pressure, pushing forward with intent around the middle of the first half. However, the momentum shifted back to United with key attacking bursts that led to their two goals. Towards the end of the half, both teams had their moments, but United consistently looked more dangerous during their attacking phases.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: Casemiro has been the standout player for Manchester United, not only scoring but also contributing with an assist and displaying a commanding presence in midfield. Matheus Cunha has also impressed with his goal and constant threat in the final third. For Brighton, Danny Welbeck has been a focal point in attack, managing a shot on target, while Maxim De Cuyper has shown creativity with key passes, but they’ve lacked the cutting edge to trouble United’s defense significantly.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: Manchester United have been effective in exploiting spaces in the final third, with frequent entries into dangerous areas leading to big chances. Their ability to win possession in advanced positions has helped them maintain pressure. Brighton, on the other hand, have dominated possession and shown strength in aerial duels, but their inability to create big chances or test United’s goalkeeper more often has been their downfall. Defensively, United have held firm despite Brighton’s numerous penalty area entries.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Manchester United will likely focus on maintaining their lead while looking to capitalize on counter-attacking opportunities. Brighton must adopt a more aggressive approach to break down United’s defense, possibly introducing fresh legs to inject pace and creativity. The away side needs to improve their finishing if they are to mount a comeback, while United will aim to manage the game and potentially add to their tally with key players in form.
